channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: The Virtual OS Museum lets you relive over 600 operating systems right on your desktop
item.description: The Virtual OS Museum isn't a physical place, it's a collection of over 1,700 distinct installations of over 600 operating systems for over 250 platforms that you can download and run via...

item.title: How I stopped 100 goroutines from hammering my gRPC server — Loom Part 2
item.description: This is Part 2 of my series building Loom. 👉 Missed Part 1? Read it here Today: Reflection cache, stampede protection, and the deadlock that kept me up until 11 PM. The problem When 50 goroutines...

item.title: Building a Financial Risk Intelligence Agent That Learns from Every Investigation
item.description: Enhancing Fraud Investigations Through Memory-Powered AI Agents Traditional fraud detection systems are excellent at identifying suspicious transactions, but they have one major limitation: They...

item.title: GA Power Pages AI Skills Under Test: Better Scaffolding, Same Local Auth Wall
item.description: TL;DR: building a Power Pages SPA with the GitHub Copilot AI skills for Power Platform, the portal handles authentication in a completely different way from the previous article — custom React...

item.description: Episode 6: Let the Wookiee Win — R2 Moves! "Would Somebody Get This Big Walking Carpet Out of My Way?" 🦾 Han Solo enters the workshop with the energy of someone who has been waiting since Episode...

item.title: Dell’s new XPS 14 is better in almost every way
item.description: The 2026 XPS 14 is the best premium laptop we've seen from Dell in a while, with incredible build quality in a thin machine and good performance thanks to Intel's Core Ultra Series 3 "Panther...

item.title: Kill some time with these much needed distractions
item.description: Constantly being plugged into the news grind is mentally exhausting. Sometimes we just need to take a break, unwind, and do something fun. That’s why we’ve built up a collection of distracting...

item.title: AI ‘content creators’ are getting harder to spot
item.description: This is The Stepback, a weekly newsletter breaking down one essential story from the tech world. For more on AI confusion, follow Robert Hart. The Stepback arrives in our subscribers' inboxes at...

item.title: Sponsors especially OPENAI CODEX voucher usage for codex - openAI challange
JMGO’s N3 Ultimate projector is the new portable 4K champ
item.description: Sorry Anker: JMGO now makes my favorite flagship portable projector. The N3 Ultimate is an excellent portable 4K projector that defeats moderate ambient light at severe placement angles and can...

item.title: OpenAI unveils Lockdown Mode to protect sensitive data from prompt injection attacks
item.description: Even with Lockdown Mode, ChatGPT could be still vulnerable to prompt injections, but the goal is to reduce the likelihood that sensitive data gets shared in the process.

item.title: What to expect from WWDC 2026: Siri’s highly anticipated revamp and Apple Intelligence updates
item.description: Apple's WWDC nears: Here's what you can look forward to.

item.title: The first Story-Rich showcase was packed with narrative-driven games
item.description: Fellow Traveller, the publisher behind games like Titanium Court and 1000xResist, just wrapped up its Story-Rich Showcase, which featured a bunch of narrative-driven indie games. With more than 20...

item.title: GOG apologizes for emailing people Nazi symbols
item.description: GOG sent a newsletter about the game The End of the Sun on June 5th that included symbols associated with the Nazi SS. The Steam competitor issued a statement attributing the inclusion to a...

item.title: Sriram Krishnan is leaving his role as White House AI advisor
item.description: Krishnan is reportedly starting a new institution to continue shaping Trump's AI policy.

item.title: The cutest games from the Wholesome Direct 2026 showcase
item.description: Every year at Summer Game Fest, nestled in between the splashy blockbuster showcases, the Wholesome Direct provides a nice change of pace. It's similarly packed with games - this year's edition...

item.title: The Trump administration might take an equity stake in OpenAI
item.description: President Donald Trump said he's discussing deals "where the American people can benefit from the success of AI."

item.title: Summer Game Fest 2026: All the news from gaming’s busiest week
item.description: Get ready for some gaming news. It’s officially June, which means splashy new events from PlayStation, Xbox, gaming hype man Geoff Keighley, and more. PlayStation kicked things off with a big...

item.title: 4K Blu-rays are three for $33 ahead of Father’s Day
item.description: If you know a dad with a predilection for physical media, he might really dig what you buy for him with this deal at Gruv. Ahead of Father’s Day on June 21st, the online seller (operated by...

item.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
item.description: OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks. The feature is primarily...

item.title: Crypto-Funded Chinese Peptide Labs Are Booming
item.description: Plus: Hackers use Meta’s AI bots to hack Instagram accounts, Anthropic helps NSA hackers, a decades-long GPS satellite mystery may have been solved, and more.

item.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
item.description: A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping...

item.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited...

item.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
item.description: Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video,...
